rawCommits.subject: fix(stats): wire token persistence + close cost attribution gaps (#5)
rawCommits.body:
* fix(stats): wire persist_task_output to task_completed/task_failed

Closes a refactor-introduced gap: `persist_task_output` in
`apps/aura-os-server/src/persistence.rs` was dead code with zero
callers, despite its docstring saying it should be called when a task
completes or fails. As a result, tokens accumulated in
`task_output_cache` by `update_usage_cache` on every harness
`assistant_message_end`/`token_usage` event were silently
discarded — never written to `tasks.total_input_tokens` /
`total_output_tokens` in aura-storage. This is the primary cause of
Neo's 'Tokens stat shows 0' report.

Both terminal task-event handlers (`task_completed` and
`task_failed`) now call a new `persist_cached_task_output` helper
that drains the cached entry for the task, hands it to
`persist_task_output`, and removes it from the in-memory map so
memory doesn't grow unbounded across task completions.

`persist_task_output` itself was already correctly built to write
tokens, files_changed, and live output via `update_task` +
`create_event` — only the wiring was missing.

Pairs with a follow-up aura-storage stats SQL change so the
dashboard's "Tokens" stat reads from `tasks` (where tokens now
land) instead of `sessions` (where nothing writes them in the
current architecture).

* fix(stats): stamp aura-* attribution on summary calls + tool sessions

Two flows that previously made router calls without aura-* headers,
causing their cost to be excluded from per-project aggregation:

* generate_session_summary now stamps x-aura-session-id,
  x-aura-project-id, and x-aura-agent-id on the LLM call. Caller
  updated to thread project_id + agent_id from the URL path.

* project_tool_session_config (used by spec-generate and task-extract
  harness sessions) now resolves and populates aura_org_id from the
  project's org. session_id stays None — these are utility tool
  sessions without a live storage session — and the loosened
  aura-router accepts project_id alone for cost attribution.
rawCommits.cleanSubject: wire token persistence + close cost attribution gaps (#5)

rawCommits.subject: fix(stats): wire files_changed cache + merge per-turn diffs (Lines stat) (#6)
rawCommits.body:
* feat(protocol): add per-path line diffs to FilesChanged

Adds a new `diffs: Vec<FileDiff>` field to `FilesChanged` that runs
alongside the existing `created` / `modified` / `deleted` path lists.
Each `FileDiff` carries `path`, `lines_added`, and `lines_removed`.

Backward-compat by design:
* `#[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Vec::is_empty")]` keeps the
  wire format byte-identical when no diffs are computed, so older
  decoders that only know about the path lists keep working untouched.
* `FilesChanged` keeps its existing `Default` derive — every consumer
  that builds the struct via `..Default::default()` continues to
  compile without changes.
* `is_empty()` semantics intentionally unchanged: an emit-only diff
  array with no path entries is treated as empty so the live UI's
  "did anything change this turn?" check keeps the old behavior.

Also re-exports `FileDiff` from the crate root next to the existing
`FilesChanged` re-export so harness-side consumers don't need to
import from `aura_protocol::server::FileDiff`.

Pairs with the upcoming aura-harness commit that populates these
diffs from the harness's `edit_file` line-count math, and the
aura-os-server commit that reads them into the persisted task cache.

* feat(stats): populate per-task files_changed cache from assistant events

Closes the dashboard's "Lines = 0" gap end-to-end: the persistent
side of the dev-loop's `task_output_cache.files_changed` field has
documented `Populated from assistant_message_end` semantics since the
streaming refactor, but no production code path was actually writing
the event payload into the cache. As a result `cached.files_changed`
was always empty, `persist_task_output`'s files_changed branch never
fired, `tasks.files_changed` JSONB stayed null, and the per-project
stats endpoint summed 0 lines no matter how much code the agent
wrote.

This commit wires the canonical signal (#1 in the doc-comment on
`CachedTaskOutput.files_changed`):

* `record_event_side_effects` now invokes a new `record_files_changed`
  helper alongside `update_usage_cache` whenever an
  `assistant_message_end` event carries a `task_id`. Other events in
  the existing match arm (`token_usage` / `usage` / `session_usage`)
  still skip the files-changed branch — only `assistant_message_end`
  carries the cumulative `FilesChanged` payload from the harness.
* `record_files_changed` extracts the `created` / `modified` /
  `deleted` path lists, joins each entry against the new `diffs`
  array (populated by the harness's `edit_file` line-counting path),
  and writes a typed `Vec<StorageTaskFileChangeSummary>` into the
  cache. Paths without a matching diff entry stay at 0/0 — the
  dashboard reads zero counts as "unknown", not "no change".
* The summary build logic is extracted into a pure function
  `build_files_changed_summary` so it can be unit-tested without
  spinning up an `AppState` (heavyweight in this crate). Four new
  tests cover the contract: path-list grouping, diff-by-path join,
  empty-input short-circuit, and clamp-on-overflow for pathological
  `lines_added`/`lines_removed` values that exceed `u32::MAX`.

Idempotency: the cache field is overwritten (not appended) on every
`assistant_message_end` so out-of-order or retried event delivery is
harmless. The harness emits the cumulative net effect once per turn —
there is no incremental-append semantics to preserve.

Signal #2 in the doc-comment (recovery via `tool_call_completed`
events when a runtime adapter ships an empty `FilesChanged`) is
intentionally still not wired here — current adapters all emit a
populated `FilesChanged`, so the fallback adds risk without value
today. If a future adapter regresses, signal #2 can land as a
follow-up that reuses the same `StorageTaskFileChangeSummary`
target shape.

* fix(stats): merge per-turn files_changed instead of overwriting

Closes a real bug introduced by the previous "populate per-task
files_changed cache" commit (84d7ec2d): the cache field was being
*overwritten* on every assistant_message_end, but the harness emits
that event per-turn, not cumulatively. So a multi-turn task that
edited src/lib.rs in turn 1 and src/main.rs in turn 2 would persist
only the turn-2 entry — the turn-1 lib.rs edit silently disappeared.

Multi-turn execution is the dominant pattern in dev-loop tasks, so
under the original wiring the dashboard's Lines stat would routinely
under-count by a turn-count multiple even when the data was being
emitted correctly upstream.

This commit replaces the overwrite with a per-path merge that mirrors
the within-turn collapse rules aura-agent already enforces in
`record_file_change`. Specifically:

* `merge_file_change(target, incoming)` — adds the incoming summary
  to the cache vector; if the path already exists the rows are
  collapsed (op via `collapse_op`, lines summed via saturating_add).
* `collapse_op(existing, incoming)` — pure function returning the
  post-merge `op` string. Mirrors aura-agent's kind transition table
  one-to-one:
    - Create + Modify  → Create
    - Create + Delete  → None (entry dropped — net effect "no file")
    - Modify + Modify  → Modify
    - Modify + Delete  → Delete
    - Delete + Create  → Modify
    - Delete + Modify  → Modify
  Idempotent same-op pairs (Create/Create, Delete/Delete) hold steady;
  unknown op strings fall through to a defensive "incoming wins" with
  a final "modify" fallback so contract drift can never panic.

Saturating_add on every line-count merge guards the pathological
overflow case identical to aura-agent's behaviour.

13 new unit tests cover the full kind-transition matrix, three
merge_file_change scenarios (insert / sum / drop), the saturating
overflow guard, the unknown-op fallbacks, and a four-step regression
case that simulates exactly the multi-turn pattern that was failing
before this commit (3 paths surviving across 4 merges with summed
counts and preserved Create kind).

The 304 existing aura-os-server lib tests all still pass under this
branch.
rawCommits.cleanSubject: wire files_changed cache + merge per-turn diffs (Lines stat) (#6)

rendered.title: Dashboard stats finally reflect real agent work
rendered.intro: Today's nightly closes two long-standing gaps in the project dashboard: tokens and lines of code written by agents were quietly being dropped on the floor before they reached storage. Both the Tokens and Lines stats now persist end-to-end, with cost attribution wired through the previously silent summary and tool-session paths.

entries.title: Tokens stat and per-project cost attribution wired up
entries.summary: Token usage cached during a task is now actually persisted on completion, and previously header-less LLM calls are stamped for per-project cost rollups.

items.text: Task completion and failure handlers now drain the in-memory token cache into aura-storage via a new persist_cached_task_output helper, fixing the dashboard's Tokens stat reading 0 because persist_task_output had been dead code with no callers.

Session summary generation and project tool sessions (spec-generate, task-extract) now stamp x-aura-session-id, x-aura-project-id, and x-aura-agent-id / aura_org_id on router calls so their cost is no longer silently excluded from per-project aggregation.

entries.title: Lines stat survives multi-turn tasks with per-path diff merging
entries.summary: The protocol now carries per-file line diffs, the server persists them into the task cache on each assistant turn, and a per-path merge prevents later turns from clobbering earlier edits.

items.text: Extended the FilesChanged protocol with a backward-compatible diffs array carrying lines_added and lines_removed per path, with serde defaults that keep older decoders byte-identical on the wire.

assistant_message_end events now populate the per-task files_changed cache by joining created/modified/deleted path lists against the new diffs, closing the dashboard's Lines = 0 gap where tasks.files_changed was staying null end-to-end.

Replaced the per-turn overwrite with a per-path merge mirroring aura-agent's record_file_change collapse rules (Create+Modify→Create, Create+Delete drops, etc.) with saturating line-count addition, so multi-turn tasks no longer under-count by a turn-count multiple.

highlights: Tokens stat no longer reads zero on completed tasks
Lines stat now survives multi-turn tasks via per-path diff merging
Session summaries and tool sessions now attribute cost to the right project
